component.name: Select
component.description: A labelled dropdown selector for choosing one option from a predefined list. Includes a visible label, a custom chevron indicator, optional placeholder, and an optional error message. Wraps a native <select> element for built-in accessibility and mobile keyboard support.
component.figmaNodeId: none — no Figma component set exists; uses text styles only

when: The user must pick exactly one value from a known, finite set of options
The option list is too long for radio buttons (more than 4–5 options)
Mobile usability is a priority — native selects open the platform picker

patterns.name: form-field-stack
patterns.description: A vertical series of labelled inputs inside a Card, with Select used for option sets and TextField for freeform entries.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using Select for freeform text entry
antiPatterns.reason: Select only accepts predefined options; typed input is silently ignored.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use TextField for any freeform input.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using Select for boolean or two-option choices
antiPatterns.reason: Two-option selects are harder to scan than a pair of radio buttons or a Checkbox.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Checkbox for a single on/off toggle; use radio inputs for two mutually exclusive choices.

antiPatterns.reason: The label is required for accessibility — the native <select> needs a programmatic label.
antiPatterns.alternative: Always pass a visible label string.

purpose.default: Standard dropdown with label. Error state is controlled via the error prop.

states.description: Resting state. Neutral border and chevron.
Brand-colour border to indicate the active control.
Error-colour border and a below-select error message. Set via the error prop.
Muted text, border, and chevron; cursor not-allowed; interaction blocked natively.

accessibility.notes: Implements the WAI-ARIA combobox-with-listbox-popup pattern, so focus management is part of the contract: when the dropdown opens, focus must move into the listbox to the selected option (or the first option when none is selected); when it closes via Escape, selection, or click-outside, focus returns to the trigger button. Options are reachable with Arrow Up/Down inside the open listbox, not Tab — listbox options use tabIndex=-1. An implementation that leaves focus on the trigger after opening, or makes options Tab-navigable, is incorrect.
ariaAttributes: aria-haspopup="listbox" (declares the popup type — always present)
aria-expanded (true when dropdown is open, false when closed)
aria-controls (points to the listbox element id)
aria-describedby (points to error message element when error prop is set)
aria-invalid (set to true when error prop is set)
aria-required (mirrors the required prop; omitted when not set)

keyboardInteractions.action: Closes the option list without changing value
